content:
Keystrokes, slow-pokes, puppy dog tails # We’re all waiting. The pot has started to boil. Its slow, a slow roll, and the air and the water fidget themselves into a mist. Its hard not to think that there is someone at the top, orchestrating and fat-fingering their way into greater societal dis-ease. You can feel it, everywhere, and yet when you go outside and walk around and do your everyday things -nothing is wrong. Its just made up. Just a feeling.
The internet is a wild instrument and the effect it has on mankind is something we’re starting to understand but individually we feel above it. I know what cognitive dissonance is, I know what propaganda is, I know what implicit bias is, and I will not suffer its effects because I am smarter, more alert and will not fall where the rest of humanity does, because I am better than them. This is what we all think. Right? Its wrong, but its how the majority of people think. Why is that? Some people will attribute it to their habits, or their culture, how they look, maybe their parents, possibly their skin color. Its some rough version of: When God put me together, he cared more than when he put you together. This is ego and it is lying. Its so powerful though. You are susceptible, you are being influenced, you are being fooled. You cannot escape. Isn’t that wild?
The best you can do, is slow down. If you’re thorough and lucky, you can slow down enough to ask yourself why you think the way you do, and realize at the bottom of your argument is just emotion. Because you feel like it. It won’t win you an argument, but at least its honest. How can it be that the disgust people feel for Donald Trump, is the same force that put him in that position? Politics is a dirty, disgusting business. Watching the world twist and wriggle through the Trump presidency has changed a lot. Every institution is suspect. Nothing is sacred. But the story is the same, the politicians don’t care, they won’t save you, and if you think they will you’ve managed to lie to yourself for another term. How is that possible?
The left is starving for religion, and the right is sure their religion is what everyone needs. This need to fight a dragon and their hatred of Trump is what lets the left look past morality with their two tickets for suicidal empathy and go “Yeah, this is a good idea”. The left used to be the party of empathy, of compassion and understanding, and now its the party of censorship and uncensored sex. Watching the chairs get switched around is like waking up in bizarro world. The best parts about the left has been exchanged with the worst parts of the right 20 years ago. How did that happen?
When Trump became president the first time, the left had a real problem. He was taking up all the air time with his wild rhetoric and just making a mockery of them by dragging them through the mud. This lowered the office of the presidency to something akin to a reality TV show, but it was effective and the people loved it. The media could have taken the high road and they tried, but they couldn’t hold out. They were mad and wanted to fight back, and now we have a media that is covered in mud. I wish they held out and never got into the mud pit. I think we would be in a different position.
Culture is in a pretty bad spot. We need to start talking with each other instead of talking past each other. Stop mind reading and fortune telling because you don’t know. People aren’t robots. Censorship, PC culture, self censorship, these things are in the way of having real conversations. How have colleges become so bad at censoring speakers? Politics getting its tendrils into the minds of the youth is perverted, whichever way its pulling, and thats mostly the fault of the parents and young educators. An alien invasion probably won’t bring us together but comedy will. Things are starting the lighten up and comedy is coming back. That means the nation is starting to heal. Its slow, and after this Trump term it’ll be exciting to see how that changes things. Sometimes I think that if we took on a more British approach to politics we’d be in a better place. Meaning when the party in power flips, instead of undoing all the stuff the other side did, we just move forward. Maybe thats better? Its hard to tell.
title: The Weight of Freedom Fried in Duck Fat and Cat Naps

content:
Bricks and Minifigs: Healing our broken culture through corporate hate # Whatever happened to the world? Why are we like this? What a mess. We’ve got a war in Iran, gas costs $4+, pork and beef doubled in cost. AND! Bricks and Minifigs are scumbags trying to scumbag their way into throwing a kind and bumbling youtuber, Reckless Ben, in prison.
If you haven’t been on social media for a few weeks, you may have missed the ongoing saga - Reckless Ben vs Bricks and Minifigs. A david vs goliath story where an old, dying man tries to sell his cherished Star Wars LEGO collection to pay for bills. The collection is the largest one in the world and happens to costs roughly $200K. The company was supposed to sell the LEGOs, but doesn’t and its local franchise closes. BAM corporate takes it over and doesn’t give the collection back still. A youtuber picks up the story to find justice and entertainment.
After a run around, trying to serve court papers, Ben is thrown in jail twice due to lies from BAM and their collusion with the American Fork Police (weird name but an actual Utah police company). Currently Ben is in Mexico, while BAM is trying to sue him for the way they mishandled this fiasco. The entire world hates Bricks and Minifigs now, and they could have just given the old man his LEGOs back and given him the money they owed him. It could have been finished with real quick, but they wanted to be the big dog and now there isnt a good way to recover from this. Truly a tragedy in PR for this company, for the Fork Police, for the Mormon church (they’re all Mormons) and maybe even for LEGO.
Unfortunately, some onlookers have decided to try and fight the company in real life. They’re going to local stores and fighting staff, harassing customers, calling in bomb threats. Mainly vandalism and vigilantism, which isn’t helping anything, but it does show how strongly the public feels about this story. This is something America has been yearning for, for years. A reason to come together. A common enemy. An apolitical story and it lets everyone watching hate equally. It’s bringing people together from all walks of life and both sides of the political aisle, and in a way, healing the divide we have in our culture. It’s long overdue, and an odd thing to come together over.
It would be a dream if LEGO would step in and find a way to return the LEGO sets to their rightful owner, and pay what they’re worth. A GoFundMe was made for the family and its exceeded the $200K, which is great, but the story has changed into a battle against corruption and corporate greed. All of the world is praying to see BAM crash and burn. Theres also some insinuation of the Mormon mafia, since BAM and the Utah police are mostly Mormon, I wouldn’t be surprised.
If I were Bricks and Minifigs, I would Pay the family $500K, and give him his full lego collecition back. Drop the cases with Ben, and release a lengthy response in video and writing. Both the CEO and COO need to participate in this with their egos tucked between their legs. The evidence of their bad behavior is on video, from the beginning, and its not good. So the level of prostration required for forgiveness is at its peak. That would generate the best possible outcome in terms of public perception and moral standing and possibly lead to them having a future in business. The narrative they’re trying to run with is not available. If they had done things differently, maybe it would have a chance, their current strategy, but its moot due to the documentation from Ben and from the police. Y’all are fucked. The cheapest thing you could do is my idea. Its also the best.
Godspeed to Ben, and if BAM reads this, you can have that idea free.
If you want to go the extra mile, in the apology video, be completely honest and say why you acted the way you did at each step of this boondoggle of an event.
Bricks and Minifigs
Reckless Ben
Legal Mindset opinion
penguin0 opinion
title: Welcome to the Last Place on the Internet
